original_hypothesis.title: TaikoL2EIP1559Configurable Owner Can Disable Dynamic Basefee via gasExcess=0
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: TaikoL2EIP1559Configurable.setConfigAndExcess allows the owner to set gasExcess to 0, which permanently disables the EIP-1559 dynamic base fee (basefee returns 1 wei). In _calc1559BaseFee, the check `if (gasExcess > 0)` skips all computation when gasExcess is 0, and `_parentGasUsed` is only added when gasExcess > 0, so gasExcess can never organically recover. Alternatively, setting gasExcess to type(uint64).max spikes basefee to maximum, making L2 transactions prohibitively expensive.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The owner has unrestricted ability to manipulate the core economic parameter of the L2 network. Setting gasExcess=0 creates an irreversible state where basefee is permanently 1 wei.

proof_of_signal.pattern: `if (gasExcess > 0)` gates all basefee computation; when gasExcess=0, excess never accumulates, basefee permanently returns 1

preconditions: Owner (via cross-chain governance) calls setConfigAndExcess with _newGasExcess = 0
OR attacker compromises the cross-chain owner account

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: gasExcess=0 creates irreversible state; no minimum gasExcess validation

operational_questions: What are the economic consequences of basefee=1 on L2?
Could this enable spam attacks that degrade L2 performance?
Is there monitoring for gasExcess changes?
validation_requirements: Verify gasExcess cannot recover from 0 through normal anchor operations

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Compromise cross-chain owner or governance mechanism
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: gasExcess becomes 0; all subsequent anchors produce basefee=1
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: L2 network becomes vulnerable to spam with minimal cost

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Network spam enabled at minimal cost; potential chain congestion
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Requires owner to call setConfigAndExcess with non-zero gasExcess

execution_requirements_analysis.governance_tokens_needed: Governance control or owner key compromise

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: gasExcess set to 0 or extreme values
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: basefee dropping to 1 across all blocks

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
